title: IDEAS Podcast #4: Loneliness, Isolation and Digital Health
author: Tyrrell, Chandler
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
In the 4th episode of the RISE@ATU supported IDEAS Podcast Series, Chandler Tyrrell, a PhD student on the IDEAS programme, shares insights from his cutting-edge research on loneliness, isolation and digital health. 
 This study explores how adults on the margins of society experience social exclusion and how digitalisation and technological interventions (e.g. smart phones, wearable and connected medical devices) can mitigate against or compound health outcomes for people experiencing multi-dimensional social exclusion. 
 IDEAS (Innovation, Digitalisation, Enterprise and Society) is a Postgraduate Research Training Programme (PRTP) funded by RISE@ATU through the ERDF Northern and Western Regional Programme 2021–27. The programme delivers enterprise-partnered PhD research in digitalisation, innovation systems and regional enterprise development to support sustainable economic growth in Ireland’s West and North-West.
